Testing conventions:
* Use Vitest with React Testing Library
* Place test files in a __tests__ directory in the same folder as the source file
* Name test files as [filename].test.ts(x)
* Use @/ prefix for imports

Coverage:
* Test happy paths
* Test edge cases
* Test error states
* Focus on testing behavior and public APIs rather than implement details.

```text
1	"use client";
2	
3	import { useState } from "react";
4	import { useRouter } from "next/navigation";
5	import { signIn as signInAction, signUp as signUpAction } from "@/actions";
6	import { getAnonWorkData, clearAnonWork } from "@/lib/anon-work-tracker";
7	import { getProjects } from "@/actions/get-projects";
8	import { createProject } from "@/actions/create-project";
9	
10	export function useAuth() {
11	  const router = useRouter();
12	  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(false);
13	
14	  const handlePostSignIn = async () => {
15	    // Get any anonymous work
16	    const anonWork = getAnonWorkData();
17	
18	    if (anonWork && anonWork.messages.length > 0) {
19	      // Create a project with the anonymous work
20	      const project = await createProject({
21	        name: `Design from ${new Date().toLocaleTimeString()}`,
22	        messages: anonWork.messages,
23	        data: anonWork.fileSystemData,
24	      });
25	
26	      clearAnonWork();
27	      router.push(`/${project.id}`);
28	      return;
29	    }
30	
31	    // Otherwise, find the user's most recent project
32	    const projects = await getProjects();
33	
34	    if (projects.length > 0) {
35	      router.push(`/${projects[0].id}`);
36	      return;
37	    }
38	
39	    // If no projects exist, create a new one
40	    const newProject = await createProject({
41	      name: `New Design #${~~(Math.random() * 100000)}`,
42	      messages: [],
43	      data: {},
44	    });
45	
46	    router.push(`/${newProject.id}`);
47	  };
48	
49	  const signIn = async (email: string, password: string) => {
50	    setIsLoading(true);
51	    try {
52	      const result = await signInAction(email, password);
53	
54	      if (result.success) {
55	        await handlePostSignIn();
56	      }
57	
58	      return result;
59	    } finally {
60	      setIsLoading(false);
61	    }
62	  };
63	
64	  const signUp = async (email: string, password: string) => {
65	    setIsLoading(true);
66	    try {
67	      const result = await signUpAction(email, password);
68	
69	      if (result.success) {
70	        await handlePostSignIn();
71	      }
72	
73	      return result;
74	    } finally {
75	      setIsLoading(false);
76	    }
77	  };
78	
79	  return {
80	    signIn,
81	    signUp,
82	    isLoading,
83	  };
84	}
85
```
</details></tool-use>

 `C:\Users\tcurtis\PycharmProjects\uigen\src\lib\anon-work-tracker.ts`

```text
1	// Simple utility to track if anonymous user has created work
2	const STORAGE_KEY = "uigen_has_anon_work";
3	const DATA_KEY = "uigen_anon_data";
4	
5	export function setHasAnonWork(messages: any[], fileSystemData: any) {
6	  if (typeof window === "undefined") return;
7	  
8	  // Only set if there's actual content
9	  if (messages.length > 0 || Object.keys(fileSystemData).length > 1) { // > 1 because root "/" always exists
10	    sessionStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, "true");
11	    sessionStorage.setItem(DATA_KEY, JSON.stringify({ messages, fileSystemData }));
12	  }
13	}
14	
15	export function getHasAnonWork(): boolean {
16	  if (typeof window === "undefined") return false;
17	  return sessionStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY) === "true";
18	}
19	
20	export function getAnonWorkData(): { messages: any[], fileSystemData: any } | null {
21	  if (typeof window === "undefined") return null;
22	  
23	  const data = sessionStorage.getItem(DATA_KEY);
24	  if (!data) return null;
25	  
26	  try {
27	    return JSON.parse(data);
28	  } catch {
29	    return null;
30	  }
31	}
32	
33	export function clearAnonWork() {
34	  if (typeof window === "undefined") return;
35	  sessionStorage.removeItem(STORAGE_KEY);
36	  sessionStorage.removeItem(DATA_KEY);
37	}
```
</details></tool-use>

 `C:\Users\tcurtis\PycharmProjects\uigen\src\actions\index.ts`

```text
1	"use server";
2	
3	import bcrypt from "bcrypt";
4	import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma";
5	import { createSession, deleteSession, getSession } from "@/lib/auth";
6	import { revalidatePath } from "next/cache";
7	import { redirect } from "next/navigation";
8	
9	export interface AuthResult {
10	  success: boolean;
11	  error?: string;
12	}
13	
14	export async function signUp(
15	  email: string,
16	  password: string
17	): Promise<AuthResult> {
18	  try {
19	    // Validate input
20	    if (!email || !password) {
21	      return { success: false, error: "Email and password are required" };
22	    }
23	
24	    if (password.length < 8) {
25	      return {
26	        success: false,
27	        error: "Password must be at least 8 characters",
28	      };
29	    }
30	
31	    // Check if user already exists
32	    const existingUser = await prisma.user.findUnique({
33	      where: { email },
34	    });
35	
36	    if (existingUser) {
37	      return { success: false, error: "Email already registered" };
38	    }
39	
40	    // Hash password
41	    const hashedPassword = await bcrypt.hash(password, 10);
42	
43	    // Create user
44	    const user = await prisma.user.create({
45	      data: {
46	        email,
47	        password: hashedPassword,
48	      },
49	    });
50	
51	    // Create session
52	    await createSession(user.id, user.email);
53	
54	    revalidatePath("/");
55	    return { success: true };
56	  } catch (error) {
57	    console.error("Sign up error:", error);
58	    return { success: false, error: "An error occurred during sign up" };
59	  }
60	}
61	
62	export async function signIn(
63	  email: string,
64	  password: string
65	): Promise<AuthResult> {
66	  try {
67	    // Validate input
68	    if (!email || !password) {
69	      return { success: false, error: "Email and password are required" };
70	    }
71	
72	    // Find user
73	    const user = await prisma.user.findUnique({
74	      where: { email },
75	    });
76	
77	    if (!user) {
78	      return { success: false, error: "Invalid credentials" };
79	    }
80	
81	    // Verify password
82	    const isValidPassword = await bcrypt.compare(password, user.password);
83	
84	    if (!isValidPassword) {
85	      return { success: false, error: "Invalid credentials" };
86	    }
87	
88	    // Create session
89	    await createSession(user.id, user.email);
90	
91	    revalidatePath("/");
92	    return { success: true };
93	  } catch (error) {
94	    console.error("Sign in error:", error);
95	    return { success: false, error: "An error occurred during sign in" };
96	  }
97	}
98	
99	export async function signOut() {
100	  await deleteSession();
101	  revalidatePath("/");
102	  redirect("/");
103	}
104	
105	export async function getUser() {
106	  const session = await getSession();
107	
108	  if (!session) {
109	    return null;
110	  }
111	
112	  try {
113	    const user = await prisma.user.findUnique({
114	      where: { id: session.userId },
115	      select: {
116	        id: true,
117	        email: true,
118	        createdAt: true,
119	      },
120	    });
121	
122	    return user;
123	  } catch (error) {
124	    console.error("Get user error:", error);
125	    return null;
126	  }
127	}
128
```
</details></tool-use>

 `C:\Users\tcurtis\PycharmProjects\uigen\src\actions\create-project.ts`

```text
1	"use server";
2	
3	import { getSession } from "@/lib/auth";
4	import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma";
5	
6	interface CreateProjectInput {
7	  name: string;
8	  messages: any[];
9	  data: Record<string, any>;
10	}
11	
12	export async function createProject(input: CreateProjectInput) {
13	  const session = await getSession();
14	  
15	  if (!session) {
16	    throw new Error("Unauthorized");
17	  }
18	
19	  const project = await prisma.project.create({
20	    data: {
21	      name: input.name,
22	      userId: session.userId,
23	      messages: JSON.stringify(input.messages),
24	      data: JSON.stringify(input.data),
25	    },
26	  });
27	
28	  return project;
29	}
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `C:\Users\tcurtis\PycharmProjects\uigen\src\actions\get-projects.ts`

```text
1	"use server";
2	
3	import { getSession } from "@/lib/auth";
4	import { prisma } from "@/lib/prisma";
5	
6	export async function getProjects() {
7	  const session = await getSession();
8	  
9	  if (!session) {
10	    throw new Error("Unauthorized");
11	  }
12	
13	  const projects = await prisma.project.findMany({
14	    where: {
15	      userId: session.userId,
16	    },
17	    orderBy: {
18	      updatedAt: "desc",
19	    },
20	    select: {
21	      id: true,
22	      name: true,
23	      createdAt: true,
24	      updatedAt: true,
25	    },
26	  });
27	
28	  return projects;
29	}
```
</details></tool-use>

```typescript
import { describe, test, expect, vi, beforeEach, afterEach } from "vitest";
import { renderHook, act, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react";
import { useAuth } from "@/hooks/use-auth";
import { signIn as signInAction, signUp as signUpAction } from "@/actions";
import { getAnonWorkData, clearAnonWork } from "@/lib/anon-work-tracker";
import { getProjects } from "@/actions/get-projects";
import { createProject } from "@/actions/create-project";

// Mock dependencies
const mockPush = vi.fn();

vi.mock("next/navigation", () => ({
  useRouter: () => ({ push: mockPush }),
}));

vi.mock("@/actions", () => ({
  signIn: vi.fn(),
  signUp: vi.fn(),
}));

vi.mock("@/lib/anon-work-tracker", () => ({
  getAnonWorkData: vi.fn(),
  clearAnonWork: vi.fn(),
}));

vi.mock("@/actions/get-projects", () => ({
  getProjects: vi.fn(),
}));

vi.mock("@/actions/create-project", () => ({
  createProject: vi.fn(),
}));

describe("useAuth", () => {
  beforeEach(() => {
    vi.clearAllMocks();
    // Sensible defaults: no anon work, no existing projects.
    (getAnonWorkData as any).mockReturnValue(null);
    (getProjects as any).mockResolvedValue([]);
    (createProject as any).mockResolvedValue({ id: "new-project-id" });
  });

  afterEach(() => {
    vi.restoreAllMocks();
  });

  describe("initial state", () => {
    test("exposes signIn, signUp, and isLoading", () => {
      const { result } = renderHook(() => useAuth());

      expect(typeof result.current.signIn).toBe("function");
      expect(typeof result.current.signUp).toBe("function");
      expect(result.current.isLoading).toBe(false);
    });
  });

  describe("signIn", () => {
    test("returns the result from the signIn action on success", async () => {
      (signInAction as any).mockResolvedValue({ success: true });

      const { result } = renderHook(() => useAuth());

      let returned: any;
      await act(async () => {
        returned = await result.current.signIn("user@example.com", "password123");
      });

      expect(signInAction).toHaveBeenCalledWith("user@example.com", "password123");
      expect(returned).toEqual({ success: true });
    });

    test("returns the failure result without running post-sign-in flow", async () => {
      (signInAction as any).mockResolvedValue({
        success: false,
        error: "Invalid credentials",
      });

      const { result } = renderHook(() => useAuth());

      let returned: any;
      await act(async () => {
        returned = await result.current.signIn("user@example.com", "wrong");
      });

      expect(returned).toEqual({ success: false, error: "Invalid credentials" });
      // Post-sign-in side effects should not run on failure.
      expect(getProjects).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
      expect(createProject).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
      expect(mockPush).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
    });

    test("runs the post-sign-in flow on success", async () => {
      (signInAction as any).mockResolvedValue({ success: true });
      (getProjects as any).mockResolvedValue([{ id: "existing-project" }]);

      const { result } = renderHook(() => useAuth());

      await act(async () => {
        await result.current.signIn("user@example.com", "password123");
      });

      expect(getProjects).toHaveBeenCalled();
      expect(mockPush).toHaveBeenCalledWith("/existing-project");
    });

    test("sets isLoading true during the call and false afterwards", async () => {
      let resolveSignIn: (value: any) => void;
      (signInAction as any).mockReturnValue(
        new Promise((resolve) => {
          resolveSignIn = resolve;
        })
      );

      const { result } = renderHook(() => useAuth());

      let signInPromise: Promise<any>;
      act(() => {
        signInPromise = result.current.signIn("user@example.com", "password123");
      });

      // While the action is pending, loading should be true.
      await waitFor(() => expect(result.current.isLoading).toBe(true));

      await act(async () => {
        resolveSignIn!({ success: true });
        await signInPromise;
      });

      expect(result.current.isLoading).toBe(false);
    });

    test("resets isLoading to false when the action throws", async () => {
      (signInAction as any).mockRejectedValue(new Error("network error"));

      const { result } = renderHook(() => useAuth());

      await act(async () => {
        await expect(
          result.current.signIn("user@example.com", "password123")
        ).rejects.toThrow("network error");
      });

      expect(result.current.isLoading).toBe(false);
    });
  });

  describe("signUp", () => {
    test("returns the result from the signUp action on success", async () => {
      (signUpAction as any).mockResolvedValue({ success: true });

      const { result } = renderHook(() => useAuth());

      let returned: any;
      await act(async () => {
        returned = await result.current.signUp("new@example.com", "password123");
      });

      expect(signUpAction).toHaveBeenCalledWith("new@example.com", "password123");
      expect(returned).toEqual({ success: true });
    });

    test("returns the failure result without running post-sign-in flow", async () => {
      (signUpAction as any).mockResolvedValue({
        success: false,
        error: "Email already registered",
      });

      const { result } = renderHook(() => useAuth());

      let returned: any;
      await act(async () => {
        returned = await result.current.signUp("taken@example.com", "password123");
      });

      expect(returned).toEqual({
        success: false,
        error: "Email already registered",
      });
      expect(getProjects).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
      expect(createProject).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
      expect(mockPush).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
    });

    test("runs the post-sign-in flow on success", async () => {
      (signUpAction as any).mockResolvedValue({ success: true });

      const { result } = renderHook(() => useAuth());

      await act(async () => {
        await result.current.signUp("new@example.com", "password123");
      });

      // No anon work and no existing projects -> a fresh project is created.
      expect(createProject).toHaveBeenCalled();
      expect(mockPush).toHaveBeenCalledWith("/new-project-id");
    });

    test("resets isLoading to false when the action throws", async () => {
      (signUpAction as any).mockRejectedValue(new Error("network error"));

      const { result } = renderHook(() => useAuth());

      await act(async () => {
        await expect(
          result.current.signUp("new@example.com", "password123")
        ).rejects.toThrow("network error");
      });

      expect(result.current.isLoading).toBe(false);
    });
  });

  describe("post-sign-in flow: anonymous work", () => {
    test("creates a project from anonymous work and navigates to it", async () => {
      const anonMessages = [{ role: "user", content: "build a button" }];
      const anonFileSystem = { "/App.jsx": { type: "file", content: "x" } };
      (getAnonWorkData as any).mockReturnValue({
        messages: anonMessages,
        fileSystemData: anonFileSystem,
      });
      (signInAction as any).mockResolvedValue({ success: true });
      (createProject as any).mockResolvedValue({ id: "anon-project-id" });

      const { result } = renderHook(() => useAuth());

      await act(async () => {
        await result.current.signIn("user@example.com", "password123");
      });

      expect(createProject).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
        expect.objectContaining({
          messages: anonMessages,
          data: anonFileSystem,
        })
      );
      // Project name is derived from the current time.
      expect((createProject as any).mock.calls[0][0].name).toMatch(/^Design from /);
      expect(clearAnonWork).toHaveBeenCalled();
      expect(mockPush).toHaveBeenCalledWith("/anon-project-id");
      // Should short-circuit before checking existing projects.
      expect(getProjects).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
    });

    test("ignores anonymous work that has no messages", async () => {
      (getAnonWorkData as any).mockReturnValue({
        messages: [],
        fileSystemData: {},
      });
      (signInAction as any).mockResolvedValue({ success: true });
      (getProjects as any).mockResolvedValue([{ id: "existing-project" }]);

      const { result } = renderHook(() => useAuth());

      await act(async () => {
        await result.current.signIn("user@example.com", "password123");
      });

      expect(clearAnonWork).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
      expect(getProjects).toHaveBeenCalled();
      expect(mockPush).toHaveBeenCalledWith("/existing-project");
    });
  });

  describe("post-sign-in flow: existing projects", () => {
    test("navigates to the most recent project when projects exist", async () => {
      (signInAction as any).mockResolvedValue({ success: true });
      (getProjects as any).mockResolvedValue([
        { id: "most-recent" },
        { id: "older" },
      ]);

      const { result } = renderHook(() => useAuth());

      await act(async () => {
        await result.current.signIn("user@example.com", "password123");
      });

      expect(mockPush).toHaveBeenCalledWith("/most-recent");
      expect(createProject).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
    });
  });

  describe("post-sign-in flow: no projects", () => {
    test("creates a new empty project and navigates to it", async () => {
      (signInAction as any).mockResolvedValue({ success: true });
      (getProjects as any).mockResolvedValue([]);
      (createProject as any).mockResolvedValue({ id: "fresh-project" });

      const { result } = renderHook(() => useAuth());

      await act(async () => {
        await result.current.signIn("user@example.com", "password123");
      });

      expect(createProject).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
        expect.objectContaining({
          messages: [],
          data: {},
        })
      );
      // Name follows the "New Design #<number>" pattern.
      expect((createProject as any).mock.calls[0][0].name).toMatch(
        /^New Design #\d+$/
      );
      expect(mockPush).toHaveBeenCalledWith("/fresh-project");
    });
  });
});

```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-06-11 13:47:48Z)**_

All 14 tests pass. Here's a summary of what I created.

## Summary

Created `src/hooks/__tests__/use-auth.test.ts` with **14 passing tests** covering the public API of the `useAuth` hook.

I mocked the hook's collaborators (`next/navigation`'s router, the `signIn`/`signUp` server actions, `anon-work-tracker`, `getProjects`, `createProject`) and used `renderHook` + `act` to exercise behavior. Coverage:

**Initial state**
- Exposes `signIn`, `signUp`, and `isLoading: false`.

**`signIn` / `signUp`** (parallel coverage for each)
- Returns the action result on success and forwards the email/password args.
- Returns the failure result *without* triggering the post-sign-in flow.
- Runs the post-sign-in flow on success.
- `isLoading` toggles true during the call and resets to false after (signIn).
- `isLoading` resets to false even when the action throws (error state, both).

**Post-sign-in flow** (the `handlePostSignIn` branches)
- *Anonymous work present*: creates a project from the stashed messages/file system, clears anon work, navigates to it, and short-circuits before fetching projects. Asserts the `"Design from ..."` name pattern.
- *Anonymous work with no messages*: ignored; falls through to existing-projects logic.
- *Existing projects*: navigates to the most recent (first) project, no new project created.
- *No projects*: creates a fresh empty project (`messages: []`, `data: {}`) with the `"New Design #<number>"` name pattern and navigates to it.

Note: `node_modules` wasn't present, so I ran `npm install` first (per CLAUDE.md I did **not** run `npm audit fix` — the 21 reported vulnerabilities are surfaced here for your awareness rather than auto-fixed).
